title: 1.1: Identifies C as the language doing the heavy lifting for terminal emulation.
1.2: Explains that GLSL shaders handle GPU-accelerated text rendering in kitty.
1.3: Reports that the entry point fails due to missing fast_data_types module (e.g., 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named kitty.fast_data_types').
1.4: Reports the error when attempting to run a kitten directly as a Python module (e.g., 'kittens.icat' is a package and cannot be directly executed).
1.5: Identifies specific Python modules that can be imported without fast_data_types (e.g., kitty.types, kitty.constants).
1.6: Explains that Python kittens require the fast_data_types bridge to function.
1.8: Explains that the Python/C architecture is tightly coupled by design.
1.9: Explains that Python handles orchestration tasks (e.g configuration parsing).
1.10: Explains that Go kittens operate independently of the C extension (e.g., as standalone binaries using escape sequences or remote control protocol).
